evals.prompt: Create a Drupal 10 module called `user_activity` that provides: 1) A custom service that retrieves activity data for a given user (number of nodes they authored, last access timestamp). 2) A page at `/user/{user}/activity` displaying the user's activity summary with a dynamic page title showing their display name. 3) A JSON endpoint at `/api/user/{user}/activity` returning the activity data as JSON. 4) Proper access control on both routes. 5) Use dependency injection. D11 compatible.
evals.expected_output: Custom service in .services.yml, controller with create() DI, {user} entity auto-upcasting, _title_callback for dynamic title, _permission access
expectations: Route parameter is named `{user}` (matching the entity type machine name) for automatic entity upcasting -- the controller does NOT manually call User::load() or \Drupal\user\Entity\User::load() (Drupal auto-loads when param name matches entity type)
Route file defines `_title_callback` pointing to a controller method for dynamic page title (not just a static `_title` string -- dynamic titles need a callback that receives the upcasted entity)

The custom service class does NOT extend ControllerBase -- services are standalone classes with constructor injection via .services.yml arguments (not the create() factory pattern which is for controllers)
Controller does NOT use static \Drupal::service() or \Drupal::entityTypeManager() calls -- injects all dependencies via create() + constructor (static calls bypass DI, making code untestable)
Routes use `_permission` for access control (not `_access: 'TRUE'` which bypasses all access checking including for anonymous users)
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en user_activity -y returns exit code 0
browser_checks: Log in as admin via drush uli, navigate to /user/1/activity — verify the page renders with a dynamic title containing the admin user's display name and shows activity summary content (not a 404 or fatal error, confirming route registration and entity auto-upcasting work)
Navigate to /api/user/1/activity — verify the response contains valid JSON with activity data fields (not an HTML error page or 500 error)
